title: quercus calophylla f. alamo
intro: Quercus calophylla f. alamo, also known as the Quaking Oak, is a subspecies of Quercus calophylla, a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Quercus calophylla f. alamo lie in its leaves, bark, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, as well as its ability to support immune function and promote wound healing. Quercus calophylla f. alamo can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
